FSIA Tower

* This page is in development. Come back soon.

Runway Selection

  • The preferred landing runway at FSIA is runway 31, winds permitting, as it offers an ILS approach and a quick exit to the apron.
  • The preferred takeoff runway at FSIA is runway 13, winds permitting, as it offers a shorter taxi to the threshold.

Runway Change Operations

* Runway changes must be coordinate with existing DEL, GND, TWR, DEP and APP controllers. * If possible, hold aircraft on stand before a runway change operation. * If an aircraft is already taxying on Twy F, offer the pilot the option to use the existing active Rwy or to enter the runway for taxiway direction change. * If an aircraft as already passed an approach IAF fix, an APP or TWR controller should advise the pilot of the possibility of a runway change prior to the aircraft being on the final approach segment.

Wake Separation

Arrivals (nm)

Lead J H M L
J
H 6 4
M 7 5 5
L 8 6 5 5

Departures (min)

Lead J H M L
J
H 2
M 3 2
L 3 2 2

Missed Approach Procedures

HIRO

  • HIRO is not possible at FSIA if landing runway 13.

VFR Operations

  • VFR Squawk Codes should be assigned through ES/TopSky.
Rwy Turn Altitude Remarks
13 Right Up to 1500 AGL -
31 Right Up to 1500 AGL -

VFR Circuits

VFR Departures

VFR Arrivals

VFR Transitions

Visual Reference Points

Helicopter Operations

  • Two designated parking areas located between Bay1 and taxiway alpha. Apron also available for helicopter landing with prior permission from ATC.

FSIA Seychelles Intl | v0.1
